prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Founded by Paul Nthenge Mackenzie and his wife in 2003, the Malindi cult or the Servant P. N. Mackenzie Ministries is a new religious movement based in Shakahola, Kilifi, Kenya. Although technically a Christian organization, many of its teachings conflict with mainstream Christianity. The group received international attention in April 2023 when it was uncovered that Mackenzie had instructed members to starve themselves en masse to "meet Jesus", leading to the deaths of at least 110 people.
B. The Good News International Ministries (GNIM), commonly known as the Malindi cult and previously as the Servant P. N. Mackenzie Ministries, is a new religious movement based in Shakahola, Kilifi, Kenya, that was founded by Paul Nthenge Mackenzie and his wife in 2003. Though often listed as a Christian organisation, many of its teachings contradict mainstream Christianity. The group attracted international attention in April 2023 when it was revealed that Mackenzie had allegedly instructed members to starve themselves en masse to "meet Jesus", resulting in the deaths of at least 110 people. The group, often described as a cult, is adamantly anti-Western, with amenities such as health care, education, and sports being dismissed as "evils of western life" and with Mackenzie condemning the United States, the United Nations,
C. Good News International Ministries (GNIM), recognized by its former designation as Servant P. N. Mackenzie Ministries and commonly referred to as the Malindi cult, is a religious movement that originated in Shakahola, Kilifi, Kenya. Paul Nthenge Mackenzie and his spouse established this new religious group in 2003. Despite frequently being categorized as a Christian organization, a significant portion of its doctrines diverge from conventional Christian beliefs. The organization garnered global notice in April 2023 following revelations that Mackenzie purportedly directed adherents to collectively fast to death, with the objective of "meeting Jesus," a practice that led to the fatalities of no fewer than 110 individuals. This group, frequently characterized as a cult, holds strong anti-Western views, rejecting facilities such as medical services, schooling, and athletic activities, labeling them as "evils of western life." Furthermore, Mackenzie has vocally denounced the United States and the United Nations.

Answer:

options.A: Founded by Paul Nthenge Mackenzie and his wife in 2003, the Malindi cult or the Servant P. N. Mackenzie Ministries is a new religious movement based in Shakahola, Kilifi, Kenya.
